scope: Core product loop: create -> execute -> refine. 5 journeys x 8 business-area characters = 20 theoretical walks.
dedupe_baseline: uat/runs/2026-07-16-l1 (20 findings) + accepted-gaps.md. Walks were instructed not to re-spend findings on those.
orchestrator_verified: sub_triggers/TriggerConfig.tsx + TriggerList.tsx have no real importers; TriggerAddForm reachable only via StudioTriggerCommitModal with lockedTriggerType (CM-STA-02)
'long_text' appears nowhere in src/ or src-tauri/src/; renderer handles only 'textarea' and falls through to single-line input (CM-AT-01)
scripts/templates/sales: 3 published / 8 unpublished; finance 5/6, email 0/1, research 8/6 (SR-AT-01)
lab_eval_results = 0 rows in the live DB, confirming LabEconomicsPanel renders empty (SL-LAB-04, FA-LAB-04, FA-AGY-LAB-04)
--dangerously-skip-permissions present at engine/prompt/cli_args.rs:107 and :282 on the persona execution path (SAM-BPI-01)
BLOCKED_CURL_FLAGS blocks only -o/-O/-K/-T/--proto; -X POST/DELETE and -d/--data are not blocked (SAM-BPI-06)

findings.title: Delta column subtracts scores earned on two different LLM-generated exams
findings.expected: Comparing v3 vs v4 holds the test set fixed; only the prompt varies.
findings.got: Version-scoped Measure overwrites persona.structured_prompt with that version's prompt, which is hashed into scenario_cache_key and drives generate_scenarios. Measuring v3 and v4 yields two different LLM-invented scenario sets. get_version_ratings AVGs across runs with no scenario partition; deltaVsBaseline subtracts those means matched on model_id only, while its doc comment calls this 'apples-to-apples'.

findings.title: The one exam-fair comparator (A/B) exists and is unreachable
findings.expected: A like-for-like head-to-head between two versions.
findings.got: run_ab_test generates scenarios once from variants[0] and runs BOTH versions against that same set - scientifically sound. But LabTab mounts only the versions table, and store action startAb has zero callers in src/features.

findings.title: Production learning loop and Lab improvement loop never meet
findings.expected: Manual-review decisions, real execution failures, or learned memories feed Lab measurement or improvement.
findings.got: Lab scenario generation reads only persona name/description/structured_prompt/system_prompt/tools. Zero reads of memories, execution history, or persona_manual_reviews anywhere in the Lab engine, commands, or frontend. The production side does learn, but that signal is invisible to the Lab.

findings.title: The only human-judgement input to the improvement engine is fed by a table no UI can write
findings.expected: A thumbs-down plus note on a bad scenario shapes the next prompt version.
findings.got: generate_targeted_improvements takes user_feedback built exclusively from lab_user_ratings. That table is written only by lab_rate_result via rateResult, which has zero callers; ArenaResultsView renders without onRate/userRatings so UserRating never mounts. Moot anyway: labImprovePrompt has zero call sites.

findings.title: No path to supply ground-truth input/expected-output pairs; fixtures are input-only even in the backend
findings.expected: Supply a reconciliation fixture (known inputs + known-correct outputs) and measure a version against it.
findings.got: fixtureInputs has zero UI callers. Correction to scout: even if reachable, fixture_inputs is not a ground-truth mechanism - it is pasted into the scenario-generator prompt as input_data seed material. There is no expected-output field anywhere in the fixture path; expected_behavior is LLM-invented.

findings.title: outputQuality rewards format/completeness/plausibility; the judge is never given a source of truth
findings.expected: A higher composite means the numbers are more correct.
findings.got: The judge prompt supplies only persona prompt, LLM-invented scenario, LLM-invented expected_behavior, and the output - no source data. The 90-100 band is 'accurate content' adjudicated against an invented expectation, not a ledger. A confidently wrong figure that is well-structured scores 90+.

findings.title: Tuning history and all measurement evidence are destroyed by every handoff path
findings.expected: Duplicating, exporting, or publishing a tuned persona carries persona_prompt_versions + lab ratings.
findings.got: duplicate() copies personas/triggers/subscriptions only - zero prompt_versions INSERT. build_persona_bundle emits flattened prompts only; gallery publish reuses the same bundle. Client B receives v4's text with v1-v3, every score, cost and provenance gone.

findings.title: No refinement surface at all at Starter - Lab AND Activity both Team-gated
findings.expected: An entry-tier user whose agent produces off-brand output can see how it performs, diagnose why, and verify a fix.
findings.got: Both refinement tabs gated at TIERS.TEAM; the editor renders only Design + Settings. She can retype her prompt and re-run, but there is no run history, no before/after, no score, no cost/latency. All 6 definition-of-done items unreachable.

findings.title: Events/trigger surface is TEAM-gated; Starter has no trigger management at all
findings.expected: A Starter non-technical user can create and arm a schedule trigger.
findings.got: events section requires TEAM; TriggersPage mounts only at that section, so no trigger list, add form, arm/disarm toggle or next-fire display is reachable on Starter. No in-product upgrade path is shown.

findings.title: The whole self-service trigger UI is orphaned code - no live mount at any tier
findings.expected: The designed trigger list + add form is mounted somewhere reachable.
findings.got: TriggerConfig (hosts TriggerAddForm) and TriggerList have ZERO importers. TriggersPage renders 8 tabs, none a trigger list/create tab. Only live path is StudioTriggerCommitModal, which passes lockedTriggerType and thereby hides the NL input, quick templates and type pickers. Dry-run, unattended-mode and the approval queue are therefore unreachable for everyone. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED.

findings.title: No confidence signal exists; 'On low confidence' is a label with nothing behind it
findings.expected: An execution result carries a confidence score a trigger/chain can branch on.
findings.got: No confidence field on any execution/review model. The builder offers never|on_low_confidence|always, but the runtime collapses on_low_confidence to ReviewPolicy::On - i.e. ALWAYS review. Chain conditions are only any/success/failure/jsonpath.

findings.title: Same 'On low confidence' label inverts to trust_llm (never queue a human) on the recipe-adoption path
findings.expected: One label, one meaning; low-confidence output always reaches a human.
findings.got: reviewModeToSetting() maps anything not never/off/always to 'trust_llm'. useAdoption writes it into generation_settings, which dispatch gives PRECEDENCE over review_policy.mode, and TrustLlm auto-resolves the review row so it never blocks a human queue. Builder path = always-review; adopted-recipe path = never-review. Identical UI wording.

findings.title: No account-research + outreach template reachable in a shipped build
findings.expected: Browsing Sales surfaces a template matching 'research an account, draft personalized outreach'.
findings.got: 9/12 sales templates are is_published:false and skipped unless import.meta.env.DEV; the only exact fit (outbound-sales-intelligence-pipeline) never loads in prod. Explore advertises categories outreach/growth/collections with ZERO templates. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED: sales 3 published / 8 unpublished; the pattern repeats in finance (5/6) and email (0/1).

findings.title: Brand-voice paste question declares type long_text, which no renderer handles - falls through to a single-line input
findings.expected: The voice-sample question renders a multi-line textarea accepting newline-delimited posts.
findings.got: The renderer branches on 'textarea' only; 'long_text' hits the terminal else and renders <input type=text> (single line, max-w-sm). 'long_text' appears nowhere in src/ or src-tauri/src/. Newlines cannot be typed, so the ===PLATFORM:=== structure the voice-doc builder parses is unenterable. This is the ONLY content-specific grounding input in the template. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED.

findings.title: No reachable UI can pass a source document into an adopted persona - the only input_schema renderer is orphaned
findings.expected: A run affordance renders the use case's declared input_schema fields so the user supplies the source artifact.
findings.got: UseCaseExecutionPanel is the sole component rendering useCase.input_schema fields and is imported by NOTHING. Both reachable run paths send sample_input/fixture instead. content-cascade's sample_input.source_input is the empty string, so the Run button fires the repurposer with a blank artifact - which its own error_handling says must halt.

findings.title: Approval policy is captured first-class at build time but compiles to prose, not an interlock
findings.expected: review_policy.mode='always' produces a runtime gate that blocks the action before it fires.
findings.got: review_policy IS a first-class IR field carried through promote and honored by dispatch precedence. But enforcement is a prompt sentence, the CLI runs --dangerously-skip-permissions, and dispatch only reacts once the LLM voluntarily emits ManualReview - i.e. after the tool already ran. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED at cli_args.rs:107 and :282.

findings.title: Pre-promote test runs LLM-composed curl against production APIs with no HTTP-method allowlist
findings.expected: Backend enforces read-only verbs for build-time probes.
findings.got: Prompt says 'GET or read-only only', but validate_curl_args blocks only file-IO flags - -X POST/DELETE and -d/--data pass. In one-shot mode this fires with zero user interaction. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED: BLOCKED_CURL_FLAGS = [-o,--output,-O,--remote-name,-K,--config,-T,--upload-file,--proto].

findings.title: No destructive-action boundary or tool allow/denylist in the persona contract
findings.expected: Build asks 'what must this NEVER do?' and stores a machine-checkable forbidden-action/tool set.
findings.got: Only free-prose constraints (2-5 strings) and per-capability review_policy. No forbidden_tools/denylist/allowed_tools field exists in agent_ir. Blast radius is unrepresentable at build time.

findings.title: auto_triage review mode is unrepresentable in the editable policy control
findings.expected: Every review mode the build can emit is visible and selectable.
findings.got: Prompt rule 21 emits auto_triage and dispatch honors it, but the ReviewPolicy TS union and REVIEW_MODES list only never|on_low_confidence|always. An auto_triage capability renders with NO mode selected; touching the control silently rewrites the safety policy.

findings.title: has_credential:true is set from mere presence; available healthcheck signal is discarded
findings.expected: Build distinguishes probed/Verified from configured-but-never-probed.
findings.got: healthcheck_last_success exists on the ledger but is never passed to the prompt; the LLM sets has_credential from the name list alone. Draft claims wired connectors that may be dead.

findings.title: No success/failure split on notifications, and the pref struct has no UI writer
findings.expected: Route failures loudly, suppress routine successes.
findings.got: NotificationPrefs exposes only execution_completed/manual_review/new_message/healing_issue - no execution_failed. A failed run reuses the SAME gate as a success. parse_prefs returns all-true defaults whenever the JSON is an array, and every frontend writer serialises an array - so the toggles are dead config.

findings.title: Run-failure and healing alerts hardcode use_case_id:None - a use-case-scoped channel silently drops every page
findings.expected: A configured Slack/Telegram channel receives run-failure alerts.
findings.got: notify_execution_completed_rich and notify_healing_issue build DeliveryContext with use_case_id:None. filter_channels_for_delivery maps ChannelScopeV2::Specific over ctx.use_case_id and .unwrap_or(false) - so any channel scoped to specific use cases drops failure and healing alerts with no error, no log, no UI signal.

findings.title: Alert delivery is fire-and-forget: one attempt, no retry, no dead-letter, no escalation
findings.expected: The page itself is the last reliable link - retry/backoff or fall back to another channel.
findings.got: Both delivery loops attempt once; on Err they record_failure + warn and drop the message. A Slack blip at 3am permanently loses the page for a failed production run. The v2 path never emits NOTIFICATION_DELIVERY on failure, so DeliveryHealthBadge doesn't live-update.

findings.title: Timeout/rate-limit retries restart the task fresh with no tool-call idempotency
findings.expected: A retry after partial execution must not re-apply external side effects already committed.
findings.got: spawn_delayed_retry takes continuation:None for rate-limit/timeout/usage-limit - the task restarts from a fresh prompt. Idempotency exists only at the execution-ROW level; nothing guards tool calls. A persona that posted 40 Slack messages then timed out redoes them, up to MAX_RETRY_COUNT=3.

findings.title: No always-on execution path: triggers only fire while the Tauri window process is alive
findings.expected: An armed 03:00 schedule fires at 03:00 whether or not the user is logged in.
findings.got: The only scheduler loop lives in-process, started from app boot. The headless personas-daemon binary is required-features=['daemon'] with default=[], described in-file as a 'Phase 0 scaffold' - absent from shipped builds, zero Tauri commands, zero UI. No autostart plugin, no close-to-tray handler.

findings.title: The app-must-be-running contract is never disclosed at arm time
findings.expected: The trigger form states the liveness requirement before arming an on-call automation.
findings.got: Zero occurrences of running/open/offline/background/sleep copy in triggers.json or schedules.json. TriggerCountdown renders an authoritative 'next fire in 4h 12m' with no caveat that nothing fires if the lid closes.

findings.title: A dead trigger and a brand-new trigger render identically
findings.expected: A trigger that never fired, or whose next_trigger_at went NULL, shows a distinct 'never armed / broken' state.
findings.got: get_health_map computes health purely from executions; total==0 -> 'unknown', and HealthDot renders null for unknown. A schedule silently dead from an invalid timezone looks exactly like one created ten seconds ago. Trigger-level failure is invisible; the map is fetched once on mount with no refresh.

findings.title: The prompt actually sent to the model is never persisted - only its length
findings.expected: After a wrong-but-not-failed run, read the exact resolved prompt that produced it.
findings.got: prompt_text is assembled in memory, piped to stdin, and dropped. The log records only 'Prompt length: N characters'. No prompt column on persona_executions. The Companion path already dumps full snapshots to ~/.personas/debug/prompts - the persona runner does not.

findings.title: Dry-run shows a full prompt that is NOT the prompt the real run sends
findings.expected: The one prompt the user can read equals what runs, or is loudly labelled a subset.
findings.got: DryRunReport.prompt renders in full with a copy button, but dry_run calls assemble_prompt with credential hints / workspace instructions / connector hints all None and performs zero memory injection. The real path appends memory, prior-human-feedback, sections, engine-mode directives and ambient blocks after assembly. Divergence is silent.

findings.title: Tool results persisted at 200 chars - the data that misled the model is unrecoverable
findings.expected: Raw tool call inputs and results, full, for the calls that produced the bad answer.
findings.got: ToolCallStep stores only input_preview/output_preview; the parser truncates input to 500 and results to 200 chars before persistence. A connector returning a wrong 4KB JSON payload is indistinguishable from a right one after the fact.

findings.title: Tool-call evidence is irreversibly truncated in the execution record
findings.expected: The record preserves the exact query issued and the full rows returned, so a figure traces to source data.
findings.got: Tool input truncated at parse time and tool output at persist time; both store only a preview. The SQL run and the rows behind the number are gone from the record. Raw survives only in the flat stream-json log.

findings.title: Nothing detects or flags a number that changed vs the prior run
findings.expected: A recurring report flags 'revenue moved 14% vs last run' before shipping.
findings.got: AssertionType has six text-shape variants only; JsonPath is exact string comparison with no operator/tolerance. Bulk-rerun's regressionCount is a status flip (fail->pass), never an output-value delta; comparisonHelpers only diff cost/tokens/duration. No numeric drift detection exists.

findings.title: Output-assertion guardrail layer has no UI - API module has zero consumers
findings.expected: Set a rule ('output must contain a reconciliation total') and a violating run routes to review.
findings.got: Backend commands + typed API wrapper exist and on_failure supports review/heal, but no component in src/ imports outputAssertions.ts. The only configurable quality gate is unreachable.

findings.title: Pipeline waterfall renders fabricated stage timings as if measured
findings.expected: A timing chart shows measured spans, or says it is estimated.
findings.got: For any historical run without a live trace, buildSyntheticTrace estimates stage durations 'based on typical proportions'. The only differentiator is an isLive pulse dot; the synthetic case carries no 'estimated' label and is styled identically.

findings.title: Team-memory dedup by title silently discards every REPEAT correction
findings.expected: Correcting the same recurring mistake twice refines what the agent learned.
findings.got: The team branch skips the write when a row with the same (team_id, title) exists - title is 'Human {verdict}: {review.title}'. Support review titles are template-generated and repetitive, so correction #2..N on the same title write NOTHING. The skip leaves learned=None, so the 'Learned' toast never fires - zero signal that the correction taught nothing.

findings.title: No confidence anywhere: 'on_low_confidence' is a synonym for 'always'
findings.expected: See the confidence score and have low-confidence-only routing be a system invariant.
findings.got: review_policy.mode 'on_low_confidence' maps to ReviewPolicy::On - byte-identical to 'always'. No numeric threshold exists. PersonaManualReview has no confidence column. With the permissive default every emitted review lands in the queue.

findings.title: 7-day GC silently auto-resolves the review backlog, teaches nothing, shows no badge
findings.expected: A review not reached stays open, or is unmistakably flagged as aged-out.
findings.got: A startup sweep flips every pending review older than a hardcoded 7d to 'resolved' via raw SQL, bypassing update_status - so no learning memory is written. The repo comment claims the sentinel is 'matched by the frontend', but detectAutoResolution only tests /trust_llm/ and /auto_triage/ - neither matches, so NO AutoResolvedBadge renders.

findings.title: 'On low confidence' is a phantom gate at build time too
findings.expected: The middle review option builds a persona that auto-sends confident replies and queues only uncertain ones.
findings.got: The build offers three distinct options, but on_low_confidence maps to ReviewPolicy::On, byte-identical to always. No confidence value exists anywhere in the pipeline.

findings.title: The build emits a 'minimum confidence' tunable that is only string-substituted into prompt text
findings.expected: If the Parameters card shows a minimum-confidence dial, moving it changes what escalates.
findings.got: The prompt names 'Triagers: minimum confidence' as a good parameter, then instructs the LLM to rewrite the value as {{param.<key>}}. Substitution is plain text replacement - the number is a suggestion to the model, never a gate.

findings.title: Promote says 'ready to use' while the persona is being marked needs_credentials
findings.expected: If promote marks the persona needs_credentials or drops entities, say so before the user walks away.
findings.got: promote_build_draft returns connectors_needing_setup + entity_errors; the from-scratch caller throws the whole PromoteResult away, fires 'promoted to production and ready to use', and auto-navigates after 1.5s to an editor that never renders SetupStatusBadge.

findings.title: VaultConnectorPicker empty state is dead code
findings.expected: Empty vault + a connector question -> 'No email connector in your vault yet' + add CTA.
findings.got: out.push(ADD_FROM_VAULT_SENTINEL) runs unconditionally, so if (items.length === 0) can never be true. The explanatory empty block is unreachable; the user sees one unlabeled card at the exact moment the build blocks on a connector.

findings.title: Prototype-named layout switcher + 'Let AI decide everything' ship ungated above the intent box
findings.expected: Nothing to decide before describing the job.
findings.got: Toggle row renders with no DEV/tier guard: 'Cinema' vs 'Dialogue+Cinema' (tooltips literally say '(prototype)') plus a one-shot toggle labelled 'Let AI decide everything'. Three build-mode decisions in unfamiliar vocabulary before the first sentence is typed.

findings.title: Reference context silently truncated at 8,000 chars - user never told
findings.expected: If the voice sample is too long, the app says so before building.
findings.got: MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS = 8_000; a truncation note is injected into the prompt for the LLM only. The textarea has no maxLength, no counter while open, no warning - the char count renders only when the field is collapsed AND non-empty.

findings.title: Build entry is paste-only; the 256KB file/URL attach exists but only mid-build
findings.expected: Attach back issues as a brand-voice corpus at the moment of describing the persona.
findings.got: BuildContextField is a 4-row textarea with no attach affordance. The file/URL reference pipeline (256KB, extension allowlist, SSRF-safe fetch) is wired only to clarifying questions carrying accepts_reference.

findings.title: review_policy 'always' is a prompt instruction, not a runtime gate
findings.expected: Nothing posts until approved - enforced by the app, not the model's cooperation.
findings.got: mode='always' only appends a natural-language line telling the LLM to emit manual_review; no execution-side block. Build-promoted triggers are inserted enabled=1, status='active'.

findings.title: Build prompt never sees the DB schema - only connector names
findings.expected: A persona built to query a ledger is grounded in real tables/columns/types.
findings.got: The only connector grounding is name + category + emits. No columns, no types, no row counts. build_schema_context() exists and is used by nl_query/schema_proposal, but the build session never calls it. Rule 26 resolves connectors directly with ZERO clarifying questions when the intent names a service. The generated SQL guidance is invented.

findings.title: Draft test auto-passes db_query without touching the database
findings.expected: The pre-promote test proves the persona can actually reach and read the database.
findings.got: db_query/database/database_query/personas_database are hard-listed as is_builtin_platform and auto-pass with latency_ms: 0 and no execution. For a DB-only persona every tool shows green having queried nothing - while the hook's docblock claims it 'executes each tool against live APIs'.

findings.title: No provenance contract - the build never designs a number that can be traced
findings.expected: The build prompt requires the persona to attach source-of-truth to figures.
findings.got: 795 lines of prompt contract cover mission verbs, icons, model tiers - and zero lines on numeric provenance. Grep for cite/provenance/traceable/reconcile returns nothing. user_message guidance asks for 'the final deliverable only - no thinking process', which actively suppresses the workings.

findings.title: Draft prompt mandates 'recent news' hooks with no tool that can retrieve news
findings.expected: Every personalization claim traceable to a retrieved fact.
findings.got: voice.style requires every draft cite 'tech stack, funding stage, recent news'. Template has zero web_search; tool_hints are only http_request/file_read/file_write, and enrichment connectors return firmographics, not news. The model is instructed to produce hooks it cannot ground -> confabulation.

findings.title: Adoption captures zero seller-side context (what he sells, ICP, value prop)
findings.expected: Adoption asks what I sell / to whom, so the drafted CTA has a real offer.
findings.got: All 5 adoption_questions are plumbing (credential, dedup window, batch size, score threshold, sheet). build_adoption_adjust_prompt receives only base prose + answers + connector swaps. Yet the draft stage must write 'hook + pain + VALUE + CTA'. The schema supports text/textarea, so this is authoring, not engine.

findings.title: Readiness badge shows 'Ready' while two API keys and the spreadsheet are unbound
findings.expected: Readiness reflects everything needed for a first successful run.
findings.got: getRequiredConnectorCategories reads dr.suggested_connectors, absent on v3 templates -> returns null -> falls back to connectors_used, derived from persona.connectors=['crm'] only. Any CRM credential yields 100% 'Ready', while nested credential_fields and the spreadsheet connector are unchecked.

findings.title: Batch-over-spreadsheet only; no 'research this account now' entry point
findings.expected: Paste an account, get a draft in ~2 min.
findings.got: Sole trigger is polling cron */15 over a sheet; input_schema exposes only dedup_window_days/batch_size/high_value_threshold - no account/domain input. The user must maintain a Google Sheet then wait up to 15 min. Per-account interactive volume is unserved.

findings.title: Persona bundle export/gallery publish silently drops persona.parameters
findings.expected: The parameters array travels with export/publish so a client receives an operable, re-tunable artifact.
findings.got: PersonaExport has no parameters field; build_persona_bundle feeds gallery_publish_persona the same struct. Params survive in-app duplication but vanish on handoff - the client gets values frozen into structured_prompt text with an empty parameters editor.

findings.title: Recipe binding answers never reach persona.parameters
findings.expected: Answers given in the adopt questionnaire seed the persona's parameters, so client #2 means editing a filled form.
findings.got: adopt() passes only {personaId} to sync_capability_parameters - values is never sent. to_parameter_values emits schema defaults. Her answers exist only as substituted display text; the parameters editor shows generic defaults.

findings.title: Substituted recipe promptTemplate is computed then discarded
findings.expected: The recipe's engineered prompt body, with client values substituted, is what runs.
findings.got: `void substituteDeep(tpl.promptTemplate, values, bindings)` - parameterized prompt thrown away; only title/description/capability_summary survive. Runtime composes from capability_summary alone, so the recipe author's actual prompt engineering never reaches execution.

findings.title: Team presets are read-only filesystem fixtures - she cannot publish her own client build as a preset
findings.expected: Build a multi-agent client delivery once, save it as a preset, redeploy per client.
findings.got: Only list/get/adopt/retry commands exist; presets load from scripts/templates/_team_presets/. No create/save/publish path. gallery_publish_preset exists in commandNames but has no team-preset authoring surface behind it.

findings.title: Chat fallback delivers the source document under the wrong key with no use-case selected
findings.expected: Pasting the newsletter into chat routes it to source_input for the repurpose use case.
findings.got: chatSlice sends {_chat, conversation, latest_message}; non-JSON text is wrapped as {user_input}. Neither is source_input, and executePersona is called with useCaseId undefined, so the _use_case 'Current Focus' block never fires. Grounding is incidental, not contractual.

findings.title: Recurring-schedule criterion unmet: all use cases ship trigger_type manual and adoption asks no cadence question
findings.expected: Adoption offers a cadence and wires a schedule trigger.
findings.got: All three content-cascade recipes carry suggested_trigger.trigger_type='manual'; the four adoption_questions cover platforms, voice samples, draft cap, publish target - none asks when to run. Compounding it, events is minTier TEAM and Schedules exists only as a title-bar overlay.

findings.title: 'Every Tuesday' silently parses to an HOURLY trigger
findings.expected: Plain-language weekday phrasing yields a weekly cron, or is rejected.
findings.got: The schedule rule matches 'every tuesday' but parseCron returns null - the weekly branch only matches 'every week/weekly' and the day-of-week lookup only runs inside an 'at <time>' match. parseInterval returns null, so the fallback sets interval=3600. No warning is emitted. 168x the intended run rate.

findings.title: No preview or approval choice before a trigger is armed
findings.expected: Dry-run and a review-before-publish choice offered at creation.
findings.got: Triggers are created enabled (input.enabled.unwrap_or(true)) with unattended_mode defaulting to 'auto'. TriggerAddForm exposes neither an enabled nor an unattended-mode control; UnattendedModeSection and dry-run live only in the post-creation detail drawer.

findings.title: Event payloads cannot be routed by content or priority
findings.expected: A ticket event fans out to different personas by content/priority.
findings.got: Bus subscription matching is event_type + source_id (exact/wildcard) only. Payload is carried but never inspected for routing. No priority field.

findings.title: The only conditional escalate path is a hand-written JSONPath in Trigger Studio
findings.expected: A non-coder picks 'if unsure -> send to a human'.
findings.got: Conditional branching requires condition output_match plus a literal jsonpath and expected string; commit blocks until a path is typed. Otherwise escalation is an all-or-nothing per-capability setting, never per-run.

findings.title: Nothing gates acting on n=1; sample count is tooltip-only
findings.expected: Delta suppressed or greyed below a sample floor; n visible in the row.
findings.got: sampleCount appears only inside the hover tooltip string. DeltaCell renders a full-colour +/-N with a regression AlertTriangle regardless of n - no n, no variance, no CI. A single sample renders identically to fifty.

findings.title: Hand-edited system_prompt never becomes a measurable version
findings.expected: Edit prompt -> version created -> measure -> compare -> revert.
findings.got: create_prompt_version_if_changed diffs ONLY structured_prompt; system_prompt is passed through but never compared, so a system_prompt-only edit returns None and no version row exists to measure or revert to. DiffViewer likewise reads only structured_prompt. lab_rollback_version has no UI caller.

findings.title: Diagnosis never reaches the thing that writes the next version
findings.expected: Judge rationale/suggestions feed the improvement pass.
findings.got: rationale/suggestions are declared in ResultInput and never read. Improve seeds the Athena composer with ~4 scalars. lab_improve_prompt -> generate_targeted_improvements would consume the full prompt + per-scenario rationale; binding labImprovePrompt has zero call sites.

findings.title: 'Improve' asks the user the question the judge already answered, then hands a non-coder a blank composer
findings.expected: Diagnosis-to-action continuity.
findings.got: Both Improve entry points seed the composer (autoSend:false) with ~4 scalars and the literal question 'What should I focus on?'. Judge rationale/suggestions are computed and displayed but never read in labFeedbackLoop's body. A semi-technical user must author the prompt-engineering brief unaided.

findings.title: Economics panel is structurally always empty
findings.expected: Cost/latency per version x model so 'better' can mean 'same quality, cheaper'.
findings.got: get_version_economics selects exclusively FROM lab_eval_results JOIN lab_eval_runs. Those rows are produced only by startEval, which has no UI caller. Arena runs never populate lab_eval_results, so the panel renders eco_empty forever. ORCHESTRATOR-VERIFIED: lab_eval_results = 0 rows in the live DB.

findings.title: Sandbox renormalisation makes the displayed composite ~2/3 output_quality
findings.expected: Composite = 0.4 tool + 0.4 output + 0.2 protocol.
findings.got: Sandbox cells store NULL tool_accuracy and the composite renormalises over output_quality + protocol only, so the surviving split is ~67% output_quality / 33% protocol. The one dimension nearest 'did it do the right operation' is dropped, and the remainder is prose quality.

findings.title: Baseline pin is localStorage - silently vanishes across machines and client checkouts
findings.expected: The 'this is the champion' pin travels with the persona in SQLite/export.
findings.got: dac-lab-baselines is a localStorage record keyed by persona; not in the export bundle, not in SQLite. On a second machine every version shows no baseline and no regression delta, with no message explaining why.

findings.title: A version-comparison report IS implemented but has no UI entry point
findings.expected: A client-billable v3-vs-v4 artifact.
findings.got: abHtmlReport emits 'Version Comparison' (per-version cards, winner badge, per-scenario matrix) and evalHtmlReport emits 'Version Evaluation'. ReportMode supports arena|ab|eval|matrix but ExportReportButton has exactly one render site, hardcoded mode='arena'. The ab/eval/matrix generators are dead code.

findings.title: Invisible upgrade path - gated tabs are removed from the DOM, never shown-but-locked
findings.expected: A gated premium surface is visible-but-locked with an upgrade affordance.
findings.got: tabDefs.filter(... tier.isVisible(td.minTier)) deletes the tab outright; isVisible is a bare rank comparison with no locked/teaser branch, and grep finds no upgrade/upsell UI for any minTier gate. The user never learns the Lab exists and concludes the product cannot be improved.

findings.title: Every prompt save auto-snapshots a version the Starter user can never read, compare, or restore
findings.expected: If the system silently versions the prompt, the user can view, diff, and roll back.
findings.got: create_prompt_version_if_changed fires on save, tagged 'Auto-saved'. The only Starter-reachable consumer renders each as a chart tick labelled 'Prompt v{n}' - a timestamp, no prompt text, no diff, no quality delta. Versions pile up invisibly with no restore path.

findings.title: Only history surface is explicitly read-only with no restore, and no bad-output flag exists anywhere reachable
findings.expected: Mark an off-brand output as bad and/or revert to the prior prompt.
findings.got: PersonaChangeHistory is documented 'Read-only (no restore)' - a field-level before/after log, not a revert. No thumbs/flag/rating affordance exists outside sub_lab. A regression is one-way.

findings.title: execution_config is captured, redacted, persisted - and rendered nowhere
findings.expected: Exact model, provider, base_url, max_turns, timeout, tool surface for this run, visible in the detail view.
findings.got: ExecutionConfig has all of it, the column exists, the TS binding exists - but the sole reference across src/ is a type-level Pick in ExecutionListRow. No component reads it.

findings.title: Frozen execution_config snapshot is persisted but never rendered; no identical re-run
findings.expected: See the exact model profile, budget, turns and timeout that produced this figure, and re-run against them.
findings.got: execution_config is written per run and flows to the TS binding, but no component renders it; replay only prefills input for a fresh run. Reproducing an identical execution is not offered.

findings.title: Every auto-snapshotted version is labelled 'Auto-saved' - the changelog carries no rationale
findings.expected: A version history a client can read: what changed and why.
findings.got: create_prompt_version_if_changed hardcodes change_summary Some('Auto-saved'); lab.rs:627 does the same. A ten-version history reads as ten identical rows.

findings.title: Missed-while-offline counts render on a surface separate from where triggers are armed
findings.expected: The reliability record appears on the trigger that was armed.
findings.got: record_and_emit_missed_runs persists a discarded count and publishes schedule.missed.offline. But missedCount and statusReason render only in ScheduleRow, inside the overlay-only Schedules surface with no sidebar rail entry. Real data, wrong surface.

findings.title: Weekly scheduling demands cron vocabulary; no Tuesday preset exists
findings.expected: A weekday picker.
findings.got: Presets cover Monday, Friday, Sunday only. Falling back means typing '0 9 * * 2' into a monospace field under a 'min hour day month weekday' legend. Interval mode cannot express weekly at all. The user must also pick trigger type first.

findings.title: The one field that decides voice quality is collapsed, optional, and generically labelled
findings.expected: The app asks for brand voice as prominently as it asks for intent.
findings.got: Collapsed by default, rendered below the intent box, labelled 'Add reference context (optional)'. Voice is the make-or-break input but presented as an afterthought.

findings.title: Launch-failure fallback is dev jargon
findings.expected: Plain-language recovery.
findings.got: 'Build failed to start. Check CLI configuration.' A Starter user has no CLI concept and no reachable surface to configure one.

findings.title: Dry-run shows the answer, never the query
findings.expected: The pre-promote simulation shows WHICH rows produced the figure.
findings.got: BuildSimulatePanel renders messages, manual reviews and memories only - no tool-call/SQL panel, though ToolCallList with inputPreview already exists for post-promote executions.

findings.title: persona_recipe_links.config is documented as the per-client customization slot but always written None
findings.expected: Per-link config carries the resolved bindings, giving each client's link its own values.
findings.got: Module doc promises config stores the bindings shape for future hydration; link creation hardcodes config: None, and recipe_execution.rs never reads it. The designated per-client slot is dead.

findings.title: Adoption answers reach the persona only as prose appended to the system prompt
findings.expected: Voice samples become structured, re-readable grounding.
findings.got: inject_configuration_section appends '- **<question>**: <answer>' lines under '## User Configuration'. Pasted posts sit inline in a Markdown bullet, unstructured and unversioned. extract_credential_bindings is #[allow(dead_code)] noting 'credential resolution path doesn't consult adoption answers yet'.

findings.title: 'Let AI decide everything' silently disables the review and memory interview
findings.expected: Anything that turns off the approval gate says so out loud.
findings.got: One-shot bypasses every gate and defaults memory_policy to enabled:false. The toggle's label and tooltip say only 'safe defaults' and 'skip the questionnaire' - no mention that human review and the learning loop are among the things being decided. Default-off, hence minor.

findings.title: Score-degradation honesty is genuinely well built
findings.got: degraded_count and partial_coverage computed server-side and surfaced as an amber TriangleAlert + explanatory tooltip; Ollama's hardcoded $0 is flagged cost_unknown rather than shown as free. DO NOT REGRESS.

findings.title: The production learning loop genuinely closes - correction is written AND read back into the next prompt
findings.got: Confirmed on BOTH sides. WRITE: update_status embeds literal reviewer_notes into memory content, routing team personas to team_memories and solo to persona 'learned'. READ-BACK: get_for_injection_v2 pulls the persona+home-team tier, pack_by_budget re-ranks by decay score into a 6000-char budget, emitted under '## Agent Memory - Recent Learnings'. Not a false loop - a real one.

findings.title: Failure classification and retry ladder are genuinely senior-grade
findings.got: Single-source ErrorCategory taxonomy (11 kinds), severity, usage-limit parsing with real reset timestamps, durable RetryAt persisted to scheduled_retries surviving restart, escalating 10/20/30min backoff, per-persona storm cap, MAX_RETRY_COUNT, healing_audit_log written inside the fix transaction.

findings.title: Nothing mutates the agent autonomously in a shipped build
findings.got: apply_db_fixes does UPDATE personas SET system_prompt with no approval gate - but the whole AI-healing branch is behind ctx.is_dev_mode. Production healing is retry-or-file-an-issue only. Correct blast-radius default.

findings.title: Overlap, drift, catch-up and chain-failure semantics are genuinely senior-grade
findings.got: Overlap defaults to skip-with-signal keyed per trigger, consumes the slot deliberately so backfill can't replay it, emits a visible schedule.skipped.overlap event. Interval re-scheduling anchors on the prior fire, jumping whole intervals in O(1) instead of drifting. Startup sweep catches up overdue triggers. Invalid timezones REFUSE on both live and backfill paths. Chain hops carry chain_trace_id with quarantine + dead-letter. DO NOT TOUCH.

findings.title: Pasted voice sample survives the whole chain into runtime execution
findings.got: Intact chain: prompt instructs mirroring tone into behavior_core.voice; promote folds voice.style + output_format into structured_prompt.identity; runtime prefers structured_prompt over system_prompt.

findings.title: Honest-failure contract + real pre-promote dry-run
findings.got: Rule 7 injects a mandatory anti-fabrication clause (stop, emit manual_review, precondition_failed, never present synthetic values as real). simulate_build_capability runs the draft through the real execute_persona_inner with is_simulation=true - real connectors, suppressed delivery. DO NOT TOUCH.

findings.title: Run gate is honest, and 'all skipped' never reads as 'passed'
findings.got: execute_persona resolves connector readiness LIVE rather than trusting the cached column and refuses to run blind; the test lifecycle requires tools_failed===0 && tools_passed>0 so a fully-skipped test can't masquerade as green.

findings.title: Question budget is genuinely capped
findings.got: Hard round cap: one optional mission round + exactly one Phase-C round of <=4 questions, with a zero-question fast path for simple periodic reports. Optional reference context injected as framed REFERENCE MATERIAL (prompt-injection-resistant).

findings.title: duplicate_persona is honest multi-client hygiene
findings.got: Copies parameters/design_context/structured_prompt, clones triggers+subscriptions DISABLED, reports shared-or-skipped tools/credentials in a DuplicatePersonaResult summary. Encrypted-credential bundle export (format_version 3) means no lock-in.

findings.title: content-cascade is a genuinely senior-grade template artifact and the {{param.X}} substitution chain is real
findings.got: Per-platform voice docs in design_files, 'never flatten platforms into one template', citation-of-source principle, review_policy.mode='always' on every draft, memory split into <platform>_approvals/_rejections. {{param.aq_voice_sample_posts}} genuinely resolves, and hydrate_recipe_refs runs before substitute_variables - the plumbing WOULD carry her voice if the input widget worked.

findings.title: Drafts are written, never auto-sent
findings.got: Hard constraint 'Never send the generated drafts - the agent only writes them to the sheet.' Correct default for outbound.

findings.title: Armed / sleeping / disabled is legible at a glance
findings.got: Three-state arm model mirrors the Rust active-window evaluation, so an out-of-hours trigger reads 'sleeping', not 'off'.

findings.title: Composer schedule picker is genuinely non-technical and Starter-reachable
findings.got: Rhythm cards (One-off/Daily/Weekly/Monthly), day toggles, time field and a live human-English preview line - zero cron. Reachable via Templates adoption (ungated). CAVEAT: the bridge drops the minute (09:30->09:00) and keeps only days[0], so a Tue+Thu selection silently becomes Tuesday only.

findings.title: Accept/reject genuinely feeds the next run, and budget drops are logged honestly
findings.got: get_recent_resolved injects prior approvals/rejections into the next prompt as an override block. Memory packing is whole-entry within a 6000-char budget and logs core/active/omitted counts to the readable log.

findings.title: Actual versioned model id is written back, and the full raw stream is retained on disk
findings.got: model_used is overwritten with the CLI-reported actual model rather than the requested one, and every stdout line is logged verbatim with no purge path, readable via get_execution_log. The one intact link in the provenance chain.

findings.title: The correction learning loop is enforced by a Rust-side gate, not left to the LLM
findings.got: memory_policy is a hard gate in first_unopen_field/GATED_CAPABILITY_FIELDS - the build cannot finish without asking, and the fallback question literally offers 'Items I've already approved or rejected'. On resolution, update_status writes a learned memory and dispatch_review_action surfaces the LearnedMemoryRef back to the UI.
